title: Israel's Latest Attacks on Lebanon Kill 9 as U.N.-Backed Report Warns 1.2 Million Face Hunger
summary: Israeli airstrikes killed at least nine people across southern Lebanon, targeting homes and reducing them to rubble.
contents:
# Israel's Latest Attacks on Lebanon Kill 9 as U.N.-Backed Report Warns 1.2 Million Face Hunger

## Overview
Israeli airstrikes killed at least nine people across southern Lebanon, targeting homes and reducing them to rubble. The attacks occurred despite a U.S.-brokered ceasefire declared on April 16 and extended last week. A U.N.-backed report on Wednesday found 1.2 million people in Lebanon face acute hunger due to conflict, displacement, and economic pressures.

## Reporting Window
- Reported timeframe: April 30, 2026
- Location: Southern Lebanon

## Key Details
- Lebanon's National News Agency reported at least nine killed in the airstrikes.
- Homes in southern Lebanon got targeted and destroyed.
- Ceasefire got brokered by the U.S. on April 16, 2026, and extended last week.
- U.N.-backed report issued on Wednesday warns of hunger crisis affecting 1.2 million, nearly a quarter of Lebanon's population.
- Hunger driven by conflict, displacement, and economic pressures.

## Source Context
The @BBCBreaking account on X (Twitter) covers BBC Breaking News's subject area as documented in this repository's source manifest. The development above appears in news reporting that aligns with the topical scope this account monitors. Verification stems from the cited publication, not from a direct tweet capture (Twitter API access remained blocked during this collection cycle).
